title: Execution layer: thin-Python build-vs-buy decision (borrow patterns, not framework)
source: GitHub repo scout (4 Haiku explorers) + quant-phd-advisor literature review, 2026-05-31. Conversation: 'search quant trading repositories'.
why: The live execution layer is the project's #1 declared gap. Crux question put to quant-phd-advisor: adopt a heavyweight framework (Nautilus/LEAN) or build a thin Python layer borrowing their patterns? VERDICT: build thin, borrow patterns. Parity comes from the SINGLE event-loop discipline (~200 lines), not framework bulk; adopting Nautilus/LEAN forces re-expressing the invariant-laden pipeline inside their abstractions (a rewrite, the explicit anti-goal) + Rust/C# toolchain lock-in on Windows. Even the frameworks state parity is NOT guaranteed (latency, fill-price, timing diverge regardless).
value_rationale: VERDICT (architecture decision). Build-our-own for A5/A3/A4; borrow-pattern-only for A1/A2; this unblocks the fastest safe path to paper-trade.
scope: A5 (Adopt/core): one code path; PaperBroker & MT5Broker differ only at the order-submission boundary, both fed the live event stream (paper = dry-run on live feed)
A3 (Build-own): SQLite state + reconcile-on-restart; CRITICAL the venue (MT5) is the source of truth — reconcile DB *to* MT5 reports, synthesize missing fills, never reverse

A4 (Build-own): pre-trade risk gates + order FSM (pending->submitted->partial->filled/rejected/cancelled) + hard kill-switch + repeated-fault throttle; gates DELEGATE to survival_book.apply_risk_controls, don't reimplement
A2 (Borrow idioms): thin MT5Broker over raw MetaTrader5 behind the Broker interface; lift aiomql async/error idioms but NOT its Strategy/RAM (duplicates sizing)
A1 (Borrow-pattern-only): copy 3 things from Nautilus/LEAN — single event-loop, startup-reconciliation algorithm, and the PARITY TEST = live-vs-OOS-backtest equity-curve overlay
Anchor everything on the parity test FIRST: replay one event stream through PaperBroker and the backtest engine, assert equity-curve overlap within tolerance
recommendation: Build a thin single-code-path event-driven loop. Abstract Broker interface that is reconciliation-first (submit/cancel + order/fill/position reports, Nautilus-shaped). PaperBroker + MT5Broker swap behind it. Sizing/risk CALL pipeline/sizing.py + pipeline/survival_book.py. SQLite (separate dry-run DB file, freqtrade-style), reconcile-to-venue on restart. Parity test is the gate on everything. No live capital until Loop A yields >=1 audit-surviving strategy. Ranking value/effort: A5 > A3 > A4 > A2 > A1.
out_of_scope: Adopting Nautilus/LEAN as a dependency (forces pipeline rewrite + Rust/C# lock-in on Windows). Adopting aiomql's Strategy/RAM framework (second source of truth for sizing).

external_refs.label: freqtrade (dry-run = live code path; SQLite state)
